Generated by Cython 3.2.4

Yellow lines hint at Python interaction.
Click on a line that starts with a "+" to see the C code that Cython generated for it.

Raw output: matrix_ops_typed.c

+001: # cython: language_level=3
  __pyx_t_5 = __Pyx_PyDict_NewPresized(0); if (unlikely(!__pyx_t_5)) __PYX_ERR(0, 1, __pyx_L1_error)
  __Pyx_GOTREF(__pyx_t_5);
  if (PyDict_SetItem(__pyx_mstate_global->__pyx_d, __pyx_mstate_global->__pyx_n_u_test, __pyx_t_5) < (0)) __PYX_ERR(0, 1, __pyx_L1_error)
  __Pyx_DECREF(__pyx_t_5); __pyx_t_5 = 0;

 002: # cython: boundscheck=False
 003: # cython: wraparound=False
 004: # cython: cdivision=True
 005: # cython: nonecheck=False
 006: """
 007: Cython matrix operations — FULLY TYPED with typed memoryviews.
 008: 
 009: Key techniques demonstrated:
 010:   * cdef / cpdef for C-level functions (no PyObject overhead)
 011:   * Typed memoryviews (double[:, :]) — direct pointer arithmetic, no GIL needed
 012:   * nogil blocks — releases the GIL so the OS can schedule other threads
 013:   * cdivision=True — skips Python ZeroDivisionError check
 014:   * boundscheck=False / wraparound=False — removes bounds-check guards
 015:   * libc.stdlib rand() — C-level RNG, no Python objects at all
 016: 
 017: Memory layout:
 018:   NumPy arrays passed in must be C-contiguous (order='C') so that
 019:   memoryview[i, j] maps to *(ptr + i*cols + j) — a single pointer add.
 020: """
 021: 
+022: import numpy as np
  __pyx_t_1 = __Pyx_Import(__pyx_mstate_global->__pyx_n_u_numpy, 0, 0, NULL, 0); if (unlikely(!__pyx_t_1)) __PYX_ERR(0, 22, __pyx_L1_error)
  __pyx_t_4 = __pyx_t_1;
  __Pyx_GOTREF(__pyx_t_4);
  if (PyDict_SetItem(__pyx_mstate_global->__pyx_d, __pyx_mstate_global->__pyx_n_u_np, __pyx_t_4) < (0)) __PYX_ERR(0, 22, __pyx_L1_error)
  __Pyx_DECREF(__pyx_t_4); __pyx_t_4 = 0;

 023: cimport numpy as cnp
 024: from libc.math cimport sqrt
 025: from libc.stdlib cimport rand, srand, RAND_MAX
 026: 
 027: # Tell Cython the dtype we will use everywhere
 028: ctypedef double DTYPE_t

 033: # matmul — the hot loop
 034: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

#if CYTHON_METH_FASTCALL

#endif
); /*proto*/
PyDoc_STRVAR(__pyx_doc_3src_8cy_typed_16matrix_ops_typed_4simulate_dice_game, "\n    Monte Carlo dice game \342\200\224 fully typed Cython.\n\n    Rules: roll 2 six-sided dice, win if sum >= 7.\n    Returns estimated win probability after n_rounds rounds.\n    True probability = 21/36 \342\211\210 0.5833.\n\n    Uses libc rand() \342\200\224 a C-level RNG that returns int in [0, RAND_MAX].\n    All variables are C types on the stack:\n      cdef int i, die1, die2, wins\n    No PyObject* created inside the loop. No boxing. No GIL needed.\n\n    Speedup vs Python: ~10-30x (Python uses random.randint which is slower\n    than rand() % 6 + 1 in C).\n    ");
static PyMethodDef __pyx_mdef_3src_8cy_typed_16matrix_ops_typed_5simulate_dice_game = {"simulate_dice_game", (PyCFunction)(void(*)(void))(__Pyx_PyCFunction_FastCallWithKeywords)__pyx_pw_3src_8cy_typed_16matrix_ops_typed_5simulate_dice_game, __Pyx_METH_FASTCALL|METH_KEYWORDS, __pyx_doc_3src_8cy_typed_16matrix_ops_typed_4simulate_dice_game};

 087:     """
 088:     Monte Carlo dice game — fully typed Cython.
 089: 
 090:     Rules: roll 2 six-sided dice, win if sum >= 7.
 091:     Returns estimated win probability after n_rounds rounds.
 092:     True probability = 21/36 ≈ 0.5833.
 093: 
 094:     Uses libc rand() — a C-level RNG that returns int in [0, RAND_MAX].
 095:     All variables are C types on the stack:
 096:       cdef int i, die1, die2, wins
 097:     No PyObject* created inside the loop. No boxing. No GIL needed.
 098: 
 099:     Speedup vs Python: ~10-30x (Python uses random.randint which is slower
 100:     than rand() % 6 + 1 in C).
 101:     """
 102:     cdef int i
+103:     cdef int wins = 0
  __pyx_v_wins = 0;

 104:     cdef int die1, die2
 105: 
+106:     srand(42)  # fixed seed for reproducibility
  srand(42);

 107: 
+108:     with nogil:
  {
      PyThreadState * _save;
      _save = PyEval_SaveThread();
      __Pyx_FastGIL_Remember();
      /*try:*/ {
/* … */
      /*finally:*/ {
        /*normal exit:*/{
          __Pyx_FastGIL_Forget();
          PyEval_RestoreThread(_save);
          goto __pyx_L5;
        }
        __pyx_L5:;
      }
  }

        __pyx_t_1 = __pyx_v_n_rounds;
        __pyx_t_2 = __pyx_t_1;
        for (__pyx_t_3 = 0; __pyx_t_3 < __pyx_t_2; __pyx_t_3+=1) {
          __pyx_v_i = __pyx_t_3;

+110:             die1 = rand() % 6 + 1
          __pyx_v_die1 = ((rand() % 6) + 1);

+111:             die2 = rand() % 6 + 1
          __pyx_v_die2 = ((rand() % 6) + 1);

+112:             if die1 + die2 >= 7:
          __pyx_t_4 = ((__pyx_v_die1 + __pyx_v_die2) >= 7);
          if (__pyx_t_4) {
/* … */
          }
        }
      }

+113:                 wins += 1
            __pyx_v_wins = (__pyx_v_wins + 1);

 114: 
+115:     return <double>wins / n_rounds
  __pyx_r = (((double)__pyx_v_wins) / ((double)__pyx_v_n_rounds));
  goto __pyx_L0;
